div.p: Problem: Sequential dispatch lets slow consumers block fast ones Solution: Per-consumer buffered channels isolate delivery

g.text: C2 throughput | Blocked by C1 | Independent of C1
Slow consumer impact | Affects ALL consumers | Isolated to self
Memory overhead | None | ~10 msgs × consumer count
Per-consumer goroutines with buffered channels decouple delivery from processing speed:
• Fast consumers (C2) process at their own pace — not blocked by slow peers
• Slow consumers (C1) fill their buffer; when full, messages drop (backpressure signal)
This pattern appears in: Kafka consumer prefetch, RabbitMQ prefetch_count, SQS MaxNumberOfMessages

text.tspan: Per-consumer goroutines with buffered channels decouple delivery from processing speed:
• Fast consumers (C2) process at their own pace — not blocked by slow peers
• Slow consumers (C1) fill their buffer; when full, messages drop (backpressure signal)
This pattern appears in: Kafka consumer prefetch, RabbitMQ prefetch_count, SQS MaxNumberOfMessages

g.text: T=100ms: msg1 → C1 done, msg2 → C2
